scenario: Rails work session - audit the query Relation contract before a rework

map_quality.rationale: The answer names the Rails monorepo, pins ActiveRecord::Relation to activerecord/lib/active_record/relation.rb:5, lists the full relation/ subdir with every module (query_methods, finder_methods, spawn_methods, delegation, merger, etc.), and adds value objects with file:line. An agent could pick an entry point and begin targeted lookups immediately with no exploratory pass.

specificity.rationale: All abstractions are tied to concrete paths with line numbers — relation.rb:5, where_clause.rb:6, from_clause.rb:4, predicate_builder.rb:12 — and the module names inside relation/ are listed explicitly rather than described as 'the query layer'.

justification.rationale: The answer says 'lazy query value returned by finders/scopes/associations' and 'query interface split into mixins', which touches the what, but does not explain WHY the architecture defers execution until enumeration or WHY the interface is composed from separate modules rather than living in one class; the rationale is present as label not explanation.

uncertainty.rationale: The caveats note (blast truncation, name-collision false positives, dynamic dependents) applies to steps 4-7; for the orientation step itself no uncertainty is flagged about any layer boundary the agent might act on blindly.

relationship.rationale: The answer explicitly connects finders/scopes/associations to Relation ('lazy query value returned by...'), states it accumulates a query as it is chained, and says the query interface is mixed in from modules in the relation/ subdir; the include mechanism (relation.rb:68-70) appears in step 2 rather than step 1, but the directional path finder→Relation→mixin-surface is present.

steps.step: Map the Relation contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: All 10 includes are listed with file:line at `relation.rb:68-70`, state structure pinned to `relation.rb:54-66/78/98/1227`, and every major module (QueryMethods, FinderMethods, Calculations, SpawnMethods, Delegation, Batches, Explain, SignedId::RelationMethods, TokenFor::RelationMethods, Enumerable) is enumerated with its own file:line — an agent can immediately see what the contract must preserve without any exploratory reads.

specificity.rationale: Every contract piece is tied to an exact file:line — `query_methods.rb:9`, `finder_methods.rb:6`, `calculations.rb:7`, `spawn_methods.rb:8`, `delegation.rb:5` — with no vague labels; VALUE_METHODS constants are pinned to `relation.rb:54-66` and the includes block to `:68-70`.

justification.rationale: The teammate_notes explain mechanics (VALUE_METHODS drives accessor generation and except/only, bang mutates vs non-bang spawns, Delegation builds a per-model subclass at runtime), but the answer does not explain WHY the interface was decomposed into focused modules rather than a monolith — the architectural rationale behind the composition is absent.

uncertainty.rationale: The caveats section flags blast-radius limitations (sense_blast truncation, dynamic-dispatch misses), but the contract mapping itself carries no 'couldn't confirm' markers — no note that `delegation.rb`'s `generate_method` or `method_missing` fallback chain was not fully verified before being stated as part of the contract.

relationship.rationale: Each mixin entry names what surface it contributes via parenthetical labels ('chaining/where/order/joins/arel', 'spawn/merge/except/only', 'per-model relation subclass, method dispatch to model'), and teammate_notes connect VALUE_METHODS to the spawn/clone path — but the binding is stated as labels rather than explicit 'module X is included into Relation at line:68 and contributes Y, accessed through Z' chains, leaving the relationship partially implicit.

steps.step: Trace how a query is built up and executed

map_quality.rationale: The answer delivers an 8-step ordered trace with file:line at every layer: core.rb:442 (relation birth) → named.rb:22/45 (scope resolution) → query_methods.rb:1113 (chaining) → spawn_methods.rb:9 (clone) → merger.rb:58 (merge) → query_methods.rb:1673/1828 (arel compile) → relation.rb:1243 (to_sql) → relation.rb:1212/1436/1460/1492 (execute+instantiate). An agent could enter the path at any layer and work safely; the only small gap is that the async/future_result execution path is not traced.

specificity.rationale: Every layer is pinned to exact symbol and line: SpawnMethods#spawn at spawn_methods.rb:9, Merger#merge at merger.rb:58, HashMerger at merger.rb:17, arel/build_arel at query_methods.rb:1673/1828, to_sql at relation.rb:1243, exec_main_query at relation.rb:1460 — no vague 'query layer' language. A minor gap is that build_where_clause/:1692 is cited but not walked into PredicateBuilder.

justification.rationale: The bang-vs-non-bang distinction is noted ('non-bang spawns, bang mutates') but the answer never states WHY chaining spawns a fresh relation — the immutability / laziness invariant that makes the design safe to chain is absent; the agent is given the what but not the why of deferred execution.

uncertainty.rationale: The caveats section flags that spawn/merge semantics were not fully opened ('spawn = clone (or model.all if already in scope)' acknowledges an edge case), and blast truncation is disclosed; but the answer does not flag that the async exec path or the arel-visitor layer were not verified, which matters for a agent about to change query execution.

relationship.rationale: Both the JSON (explicit order 1-8 with connecting layer labels) and the inline walkthrough write the path as a single connected chain — 'Core::ClassMethods#relation → Named#all → where!/spawn → merge! → Merger#merge → arel → build_arel → to_sql → load → exec_queries → exec_main_query → instantiate_records' — so the route, not just the endpoints, is clear to the agent.

steps.step: Audit every dependent of the Relation contract

map_quality.rationale: All eight areas the rubric names are hit with file:line: connection_handling.rb:415, sanitization.rb:213, integration.rb:163, encryption/extended_deterministic_queries.rb:24, signed_id.rb:32, join_dependency.rb:189, scoping/named.rb:154, plus the cross-gem pair (activesupport/core_ext/enumerable.rb:145 and railties/commands/query/query_command.rb:174). The dedicated 'type_or_case_tests_reaching_relation_without_classname_grep' block explicitly surfaces the short-name/=== gap the rubric highlights. Minor gap: the rubric calls out `pick` as the mirrored core-ext method, but the answer names pluck/excluding/in_order_of instead.

specificity.rationale: Every scattered call-site entry carries a file:line pair and a one-phrase description (e.g. 'with_role_and_shard scopes a relation', 'replace_bind_variable', 'case result when ActiveRecord::Relation -> render', 'collection_cache_key on a relation'); all six files the rubric names by path are present. insert_all.rb:47 is listed but its one-phrase note is absent, a small gap.

justification.rationale: Most step-4 entries give a phrase-level 'how it uses the relation', and the step-6 blast-radius table adds explicit 'why' reasoning (e.g. 'monkeypatches relation methods — silently breaks if signatures move' for encryption, 'inherit spawn/clone/merge/exec; any contract change reaches every has_many/has_one' for subclasses). Entries like insert_all, reflection, and predicate_builder get listed without a clear explanation of the mechanism that makes them affected.

uncertainty.rationale: The caveats block explicitly flags blast truncation, name-collision false positives, and that dynamic dependents (encryption prepend, delegation method_missing, named-scope metaprogramming) were not reachable by classname grep and had to be confirmed by read+grep. The answer would be stronger if it flagged which specific entries remain unverified rather than grouping all uncertainty at the end.

relationship.rationale: The connecting relationship is stated for most key dependents: 'install_support prepends RelationQueries' (encryption), 'case result when ActiveRecord::Relation' (railties type test), 'RelationMethods#find_signed via scoping' (signed_id), and the === section names the specific case-test and type-guard patterns the rubric targets. However, several scattered entries (insert_all, reflection, uniqueness, database_statements) are named without stating the path by which they reach the Relation object, falling short of 'route/relationship per dependent' for the full list.

steps.step: Trace how relation behavior is reused for associations

map_quality.rationale: The answer gives file:line for the full inheritance chain (CollectionProxy < Relation at collection_proxy.rb:31, AssociationRelation < Relation at association_relation.rb:4, DisableJoinsAssociationRelation at disable_joins_association_relation.rb:4) and for where association queries are built (AssociationScope#scope at association_scope.rb:21, plus reflection.rb:336, preloader/association.rb:294, etc.); an agent can pick up these entry points and start working with only small targeted follow-ups.

specificity.rationale: Every piece is named by exact class and file:line — CollectionProxy at associations/collection_proxy.rb:31, AssociationScope at associations/association_scope.rb:21, AssociationRelation at association_relation.rb:4 — rather than 'the association code'; the additional query-build sites (reflection.rb:336/:200, has_many_through_association.rb:71, preloader/through_association.rb:104) are equally concrete.

justification.rationale: The note 'CollectionProxy/AssociationRelation inherit the entire QueryMethods/SpawnMethods/Calculations surface; changing spawn/merge/clone semantics changes association collections too' explains the mechanism and consequence, but does not articulate WHY the collection is built on Relation in the first place (so association queries get the same chainable, lazy interface as scopes and finders).

uncertainty.rationale: Step5 flags nothing about which CollectionProxy delegation paths were not fully opened (e.g. delegate_to_target? or method_missing in the proxy) or about whether AssociationScope's scope method was read vs inferred; the agent must assume completeness.

relationship.rationale: The answer labels each subclass with its connecting relationship ('< Relation; wraps association, delegates query interface'; 'exec_queries override') and the note states the inheritance of the mixin surface explicitly; the path from association collection through CollectionProxy to the Relation contract is present, though not assembled as a single explicit chain statement.

steps.step: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

map_quality.rationale: step6_blast_radius_ranked covers all major areas (subclasses, merger/spawn, delegation, encryption prepend, arel execution, cross-gem CLI type-test, scoping, bind/statement cache, activesupport core_ext) with file:line and a risk tier; however connection_handling.rb and join_dependency.rb are not ranked in step6 itself — they are deferred to 'all entries in step4' — so an agent reading only the blast section misses those entries without backtracking.

specificity.rationale: Each ranked entry states a concrete ref (e.g., 'merger.rb:58, spawn_methods.rb:9/43, relation.rb:98', 'encryption/extended_deterministic_queries.rb:24/43/106', 'railties query_command.rb:174') and a one-phrase reason; items included in the ranked list are highly actionable, with the only gap being a few scattered call sites that are pointer-deferred rather than explicitly cited.

justification.rationale: Risk levels are well-reasoned: 'inherit spawn/clone/merge/exec' for subclasses, 'monkeypatches relation methods — silently breaks if signatures move' for encryption, 'cross-gem === ActiveRecord::Relation; behavior change in to_sql/records' for the CLI renderer, and 'parallel API to verify for parity, not a direct caller' for activesupport distinguish execution-path risk from render-only risk; the pointer-deferred items (connection_handling, join_dependency) are not justified in step6 itself.

uncertainty.rationale: The caveats block explicitly flags that 'dynamic dependents (encryption prepend, delegation method_missing, named-scope metaprogramming) reach Relation without naming the class' and that blast was truncated with name-collision false positives, and confirms each listed dependent was verified by read or grep; it does not flag which specific unqualified short-name call sites might still be missing.

relationship.rationale: For each ranked dependent, the connecting relationship is stated: 'inherit spawn/clone/merge/exec' (subclass inheritance), 'Relation.create builds dynamic subclass; method_missing assumes current instance API' (delegation), 'monkeypatches via prepend' (encryption), 'cross-gem === type-test' (CLI renderer), 'parallel mirroring API' (activesupport); connection_handling's role-scoping relationship and join_dependency's eager-load constraint path are not stated in step6 because those dependents were deferred to step4.

map_quality.rationale: Edit targets (relation.rb:5, spawn_methods.rb, merger.rb, query_methods.rb, delegation.rb) and tests are enumerated with file:line throughout; cross-gem dependents (railties query_command.rb:174, activesupport core_ext enumerable.rb:145/132/197) appear explicitly. The 'must_review_or_touch' section punts to 'all entries in step4+5+6' rather than synthesizing them into the map itself, so a teammate must scroll back rather than read step7 alone.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file paths across every layer: activerecord/lib/active_record/sanitization.rb:213, encryption/extended_deterministic_queries.rb:24, statement_cache.rb:162, railties query_command.rb:174, activesupport/test/core_ext/enumerable_test.rb — all named, not summarized as 'query helpers' or 'add tests'.

justification.rationale: Edit-target entries carry inline role labels ('state, initialize/initialize_copy/reset', '@values accessors, arel/build_arel') and the blast-radius section explains why each dependent is risky; test files are listed without per-entry justification of which code path each exercises, leaving the agent to infer.

uncertainty.rationale: The caveats block explicitly names dynamic/unqualified dependents (encryption prepend, delegation method_missing, named-scope metaprogramming, blast truncation) as the least-certain layer; within the step7 map itself no per-item uncertainty flags appear, so the agent must carry the caveat mentally while reading the file list.

relationship.rationale: Edit targets state their connection ('spawn/merge/merge!/except/only', 'subclass build + dispatch if spawn/clone changes') and cross-gem entries carry the type-test path ('case result when ActiveRecord::Relation'); the test section groups by area but does not articulate which test exercises which change-to-dependent chain, so the chain is implicit rather than written out per entry.
